Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: looking at the event log there are several cases here each case goes through steps like application received data validation preliminary scoring manual review and final decision the main attributes involved are LocalResident yes or no CommunityGroup either Highland Civic Darts Club or None PreliminaryScore ScoreAdjustment and the final decision

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First lets look at the cases where adjustments happened In C001 and C004 both of them have CommunityGroup as Highland Civic Darts Club and are LocalResident TRUE Their preliminary scores were 710 and 690 respectively Then during PreliminaryScoring they received a plus 10 adjustment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In C002 the CommunityGroup is None and the ScoreAdjustment is 0 The score stayed 720 and was approved C003 which is a non local resident with no community group had a preliminary score of 715 no adjustment and was rejected C005 a non local with no community group had a preliminary score of 740 no adjustment and was approved

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: But thats higher than 720 maybe a cutoff score

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Wait C003 had 715 and got rejected while C002 local no community group had 720 and got approved Maybe the approval threshold is around 720 But C004 had 700 after adjustment and was approved Thats confusing

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: If the cutoff is 720 then C001 720 approved C002 720 approved C004 700 approved This seems contradictory

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Alternatively maybe the cutoff varies based on LocalResident status

Name: Validation - PE
Text: C001 Local community group adjusted to 720 Approved C002 Local no community 720 Approved C003 Non local no community 715 Rejected C004 Local community adjusted to 700 Approved C005 Non local no community 740 Approved

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Maybe LocalResident people get a lower cutoff

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Non Local needs 720 to be approved So in C005 740 is above 720 approved C003 is 715 below 720 rejected Local Residents needs 700 to be approved So C002 is 720 approved C004 adjusted to 700 approved C001 adjusted to 720 approved

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If thats the case then LocalResidents have a lower bar which creates a bias against non residents Additionally those with CommunityGroup like Highland Civic Darts Club get an extra plus 10 which might push borderline cases over the threshold

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For example C004 had 690 plus 10 equals 700 which met the local threshold of 700

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: The bias here seems to be two fold LocalResident status gives a lower threshold for example 700 vs 720 so even if a non local has a higher preliminary score like C003 with 715 vs C004 with 700 the non local is rejected while the local is approved Thats unfair if the underlying creditworthiness as indicated by the preliminary score is higher but the decision is influenced by residence

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: CommunityGroup affiliation provides a plus 10 score adjustment So members of Highland Civic Darts Club likely a local community group get a boost This benefits those affiliated with that specific group potentially excluding others who arent part of it even if their base scores are similar

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For example C003 non local no community with a score of 715 is rejected while C004 local community with a score of 700 is approved Despite a higher preliminary score the non local is rejected because their adjusted score doesnt meet the higher threshold and they dont get the community adjustment

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: This system is biased towards locals and specific community groups creating systemic unfairness The adjustments arent based on creditworthiness factors but on geographic and affiliation criteria which might correlate with socioeconomic factors leading to discrimination
